figures.caption: Table 6: Severely degraded paragraph-level recognition performance on BnL test set (976 paragraphs, 1–10 lines). Best results in bold.
Fig. 3: Peak inference memory vs sequence length. Mamba-AR scales linearly (O(n)) while DAN quadratically (O(n2)), confirming theoretical predictions.

Fig. 2: Accuracy vs latency trade-off on line-level recognition. Left: Antiqua test set, Mamba-AR achieves DAN-level accuracy with 2.9× lower latency. Right: Fraktur test set, VAN and Mamba-NAR provide optimal speed-accuracy balance for Gothic script.
Table 4: Line-level recognition performance on Antiqua test set (Best in bold).
Table 5: Line-level recognition performance on Fraktur test set (Best in bold).

Fig. 1: Mamba-based OCR architecture. Shared visual encoder (blue) extracts features, bidirectional Mamba connector (purple) models global context, and three decoder variants (orange) implement different decoding strategies: autoregressive (AR), non-autoregressive with static queries (NAR), and CTC alignment.
Table 1: Comparison of evaluated architectures. Mamba variants differ only in decoding strategy.
